risk.desc: Potential misalignment during assembly due to loose tolerances.
risk.mit: Implement tighter tolerance control during manufacturing.

risk.desc: Risk of damage to delicate components during handling.
risk.mit: Include protective packaging and handling guidelines.

risk.mit: Ensure all cables have proper strain relief geometry.

risk.desc: Incorrect mating of connectors could lead to system failure.
risk.mit: Label and key connectors to prevent mis-mating.

risk.desc: Overclaiming of functionality in documentation may mislead users.

requirements: Non-functional stage_engine_mock with bell_nozzle_shell, cutaway_window, engine_display_cartridge slide_rail, educational label only
Removable engine display cartridge: combustion chamber dummy, bell nozzle shell, turbopump dummy in cutaway bay (non-functional)
Battery, sensor, actuator, cartridge, payload, cable, or joint modules remain removable without full teardown
Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels
Access is represented by geometry: hatch, pocket, channel, fastener pattern, slot, window, or removable cover
Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry (clamp, grommet, tie point) and routing respects a stated minimum bend radius away from moving joints and sharp edges
Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure, have finger/tool clearance, and mention keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating
Tank, interstage, avionics, recovery, and engine-display modules are labeled educational/display-only
Cutaway shows tank dummies, interstage datum, avionics bay, recovery bay, and removable engine display cartridge
Risk/verification focuses on mockup structure, inspection, interfaces, and unsafe overclaiming
Interstage and module joints show locating datums, bolt-circle dummies, and alignment keys so the mock stack assembles repeatably for review

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Recovery bay educational display dummy with deploy_door, hinge, parachute volume label, inspection window, no deployment charge or flight recovery instruction
physics_paths: Load path: Structural integrity of the dummy under static and dynamic loads during handling and transport.
Flow path: Airflow through the inspection window and deploy_door for visual inspection.

Pressure path: Atmospheric pressure acting on the deploy_door and inspection window.

Human/Service access path: Inspection window for visual inspection and maintenance.

manufacturing_strategy.support: Oriented for optimal powder bed fusion build direction
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Visual and CMM inspection for dimensional accuracy

verification_focus: Structural integrity of the dummy under static and dynamic loads
Correct operation of the deploy_door hinge mechanism
Proper fit and function of all interfaces
